commit.message:
fix(terminal): use Windows-appropriate shell and PATH on win32

On Windows, SHELL is not set and /bin/bash does not exist. Fall back
to COMSPEC (cmd.exe) and skip the macOS/Linux PATH augmentation
(which uses Unix paths and the : separator).

fix(migration): make 024 idempotent with IF NOT EXISTS

new image was built. This ensures alembic 024 is safe to run on an
already-migrated DB without failing on duplicate column errors.

commit.message:
Fix scraper: gender h1 extraction, slug detection fallback, canonical redirect handling

- Pass raw h1 text to _extract_gender so 'for women/men' span is present before brand/gender stripping
- Replace overly-broad fallback in get_perfume_urls (all /perfume/ links) with frequency-based slug detection — most common /perfume/X/ slug on the page is the brand's actual slug (handles cases like Dior vs Christian-Dior)
- Add canonical URL validation on brand pages to detect CF blocks (homepage redirect) vs legitimate designer renames (e.g. /designers/Christian-Dior.html → /designers/Dior.html); for renames, update slug from canonical and continue
- Fix seed_brands.json: update Christian Dior URL to /designers/Dior.html (correct canonical); remove duplicate Bulgari entry (was at index 15 and 95, both /designers/Bvlgari.html)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(benchmarks): add conv_cifar_v6 suite with k-means filters and 30k scaling

Build conv_cifar_v6 suite (11 configs) exploring three new directions:

1. K-means filters as alternative to PCA — k-means centroids with
   triangle activation (Coates & Ng 2012) capture diverse patch
   prototypes rather than top-variance directions.  Test plain k-means
   and ZCA-whitened k-means at 10k/20k with ensembles.

2. PCA + per-patch contrast normalization — normalize_patches removes
   brightness variation at each spatial position, focusing PCA on
   texture/edge patterns.  Previously only tested with GCN (which hurt).

3. 30k ×10 scaling — v5 showed 30k ×5 hits 77.88% (new record).
   Test whether ×10 continues improving at 30k with α=2e-2.
   Also try α=1.5e-2 at ×5 (between 1e-2 and 2e-2 sweet spot).

v5 findings driving these choices:
- ×20 saturated at 20k (77.71% ≈ 77.68% at ×10)
- More filters per branch (48×3, 64×3) didn't help
- Cutout hurt -1.2% (corrupts PCA features)
- Hierarchical 2-stage disappointed (74.62% best)
- 30k + α=2e-2 is the one lever still improving

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
security: add Envoy JWT filter + fix JWKS endpoint

Closes the auth bypass where any client could send an arbitrary
x-user-id header and services would trust it blindly.

## Envoy (ops/envoy.docker.yaml)
- Add envoy.filters.http.jwt_authn before grpc_web filter
- Validates Bearer JWT using remote JWKS from auth:8081
- Injects validated sub claim as x-user-id header (overwrites
  any client-supplied value)
- JWKS is cached 10 min; fetched async on cold start
- Public routes bypass validation: AuthService, DeviceService,
  /.well-known, /health
- Add auth_http cluster (HTTP/1.1, auth:8081) for JWKS feed

## auth-service (JWKS endpoint fix)
- Previous get_jwks() returned raw PEM base64 as n — invalid JWK
- Now uses rsa crate to parse RSA public key and extract modulus
  (n) and exponent (e) as base64url — correct RFC 7517 JWK format
- Handles both PKCS#8 (BEGIN PUBLIC KEY) and PKCS#1 (BEGIN RSA
  PUBLIC KEY) PEM formats
- Normalizes \n → newline for env files that escape newlines

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement drop elision pass in IR optimizer (#75)

* Implement borrow elision pass in IrOptimizer

Add temp aliasing infrastructure with use-def tracking per temp.
Elide Borrow instructions for copy-type sources (LoadConstInt/Float/Bool)
and single-use borrows (target used exactly once). Transitive chain
resolution via ResolveTemp. RemapSourceTemps helper rewrites source temps
in all IrInst variants.

Updated tests: replaced borrow preservation test with 4 new borrow
elision tests (copy-type, single-use, chain resolution, multi-use
preservation). All 1028 unit tests + 43 LSP tests + 245 e2e tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(scroll): only use clock for smooth scrolling, use fps.row for stepped

Root cause of 'jumps back and forth': smoothScrolling defaults to false,
but the clock row was always used. The clock predicts row transitions at
different times than the actual emulator → display row fights between
clock prediction and real position, causing visible oscillation.

Fix: when smoothScrolling is OFF (default), use fps.row directly from
the audio callback — identical to how GT Ultra works. The clock is only
used when smoothScrolling is ON, where it provides constant-rate sub-pixel
movement that masks timing differences.

Also added fallback: if onaudioprocess hook fails to fire within 500ms,
automatically falls back to setInterval(16ms) with console warning.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(#160): move core memories from system prompt to RAG-only retrieval (#162)

* fix(#160): move core memories from system prompt to RAG-only retrieval

- Remove [Core Memories] block from buildSystemPrompt() in ChatViewModel
- Split MAX_SEARCH_DISTANCE into CORE_MAX_DISTANCE=0.55f and EPISODIC_MAX_DISTANCE=0.40f
  (core memories cast a wider net — deliberately added = higher recall priority)
- Add lastAccessedAt to MemorySearchResult for sort tiebreaking
- Update searchMemories() signature: coreTopK=10, episodicTopK=5 (separate budgets)
- RagRepository: sort core results by score DESC, lastAccessedAt DESC before token truncation
- Add framing instruction to [User Profile] in system prompt
- Update RagRepositoryTest mock signatures for new 3-arg searchMemories

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#160): address code review — episodicTopK guard + tiebreaker test coverage

- Guard episodicTopK <= 0 in searchMemories() to skip JNI call and avoid
  spurious 'no such table' logcat warnings on every RAG call
- Add coVerify to lock down coreTopK=10, episodicTopK=0 contract in RagRepository
- Add lastAccessedAt tiebreaker test: equal-score memories sorted by recency,
  lower-recency entry truncated when token budget is tight

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#160): correct tiebreaker test token budget (30→75)

maxTokens=30 was below the framing header cost (~44 tokens), causing both
memories to be truncated. maxTokens=75 admits exactly one result:
- Recent fact (score=0.9, lastAccessedAt=9000): fits, budget drops to 3
- Stale fact (score=0.9, lastAccessedAt=1000): 3-4=-1, breaks loop

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: NickMonrad <nick@lab3.com.au>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
K4: Phase 1 done-task suppression fix

Stop reactivating done tasks just because the same email resurfaces
in the next scan window. Only reactivate if genuinely new activity:
- No exact link match (different email), AND
- Item date is verifiably after doneAt

Conservative defaults: missing item date or missing doneAt → suppress.
Reactivation still works for genuine follow-ups (new email + newer date).

Tested: [EXTERN] Umbau Zürich Seestrasse suppressed via exactLinkMatch.
Log: '[PHASE1] Suppressed done task resurface: ...'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Return Vec from collect_bucket_hashes instead of out-param (Closes #1376)

commit.message:
Update opencode/tools/hashline_edit.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use length-check + ct_eq for constant-time auth comparison (#153)

Replace zero-padded constant-time comparison with the canonical pattern:
check lengths first (non-constant-time, acceptable since length
leakage doesn't help brute-force a high-entropy token), then ct_eq
on equal-length slices. This avoids a theoretical null-byte
false-positive in the padding approach.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Vision WS pipe in copilot-voice; xAI/Anthropic in UI key panel; gitignore JDK

copilot-voice:
- Add copilotVoice.visionUrl setting: when configured, transcribed text is also
  sent to Vision backend via WebSocket (type:text) so Vision processes it as a
  voice command — enables hands-free operator mode from Copilot editor
- maybeSendToVision() connects, sends, then closes with 5s timeout; errors are
  non-fatal warnings so Copilot Chat insertion always succeeds first
- Update package.json and README with new visionUrl setting

live_chat_ui.html:
- Add Anthropic and xAI (Grok) entries to buildKeysList() API key panel
  (backend set_api_key already handled them; UI was just showing stale list)

.gitignore:
- Add openJdk-25/ (full JDK installation accidentally sitting in repo root)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(cli): POST-3.3 CLI DX improvements (#397)

* feat(cli): POST-3.3 CLI DX improvements

- add shared --json/--plain output handling for pack CLI commands
- extend agent33 diagnose with pack workspace and live pack health checks
- record session125 scope and progress for crash-safe resumption

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cli): address POST-3.3 review findings

- fix the packs update guidance text
- handle non-JSON pack health responses gracefully
- keep diagnose --fix --json output machine-readable
- record the PR follow-up state in session tracking

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cli): resolve --json/--plain conflict error + test_pack_registry_v1 lint

- Replace typer.BadParameter with typer.echo+typer.Exit in resolve_output_mode
  so the error message appears verbatim in Click CliRunner output (Failure 1)
- Fix N817 (SigstoreBundle alias), E501 (long line), I001 (import sort),
  TC003 (Path to TYPE_CHECKING block), F401 (unused PackHubConfig) in
  test_pack_registry_v1.py (Failure 2)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(packs): update sigstore type-ignore to import-not-found for mypy 1.20

mypy 1.20 changed the error code for missing stubs from import-untyped
to import-not-found; update the two sigstore ignore comments in
provenance.py so the unused-ignore warning no longer fires.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(packs): reject Windows-style absolute zip entries on Linux CI

Path.is_absolute() returns False for C:/... paths on Linux/POSIX.
Add _WIN_ABS_RE pattern to _safe_extract() so zip archives with
Windows drive-letter absolute entries are rejected on all platforms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
